key_insight: EasyNano turns nanobody CDR design into a fast gradient-optimization problem by directly steering ESMFold2 distograms toward a user-chosen epitope.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical, epitope-specific binder design on a high-end workstation in minutes, potentially accelerating therapeutic and protein-engineering workflows.
method: Optimize CDR residue logits with gradient descent through ESMFold2 pairwise distance distograms
Use ESMFold2-Fast as a differentiable oracle with an epitope-proximity loss
Add full ESMFold2 CA-coordinate prior to preserve framework pose; wild-type logit bias controls mutability
evidence_signal: Across six target-framework cases, EasyNano improved ipTM by up to 0.559 (0.143 to 0.702 for Ty1/RBD) and outperformed random CDR baselines, with 5.7-sigma significance for Ty1.
watch_item: Performance depends on initialization and multiple seeds can land in different local minima, so replicate runs are important and designs still need experimental validation.
paper_title: EasyNano: rapid epitope-targeted nanobody CDR design via differentiable distogram optimization with ESMFold2
